attractions.name: Bárðarbunga
attractions.extract: Bárðarbunga, is an active and productive stratovolcano located under Vatnajökull, which is Iceland's most extensive glacier, in Vatnajökull National Park. The second highest mountain in Iceland, 2,000 metres (6,600 ft) above sea level, Bárðarbunga is also part of the Bárðarbunga-Veiðivötn volcanic s

attractions.name: Búrfell
attractions.extract: Búrfell is a 669 m (2,195 ft) basalt tuya located in Iceland. It is situated in the south of the country along the western boundary of the Þjórsárdalur valley.

attractions.name: Víkingavatn
attractions.extract:
Víkingavatn is a shallow lake situated in the north of Iceland, between Húsavík and Ásbyrgi. 
Lake Vikingavatn is also called Little Lake Myvatn because of the abundance of nesting duck species and other birds.
Vikingavatn has an area of 2,4 km2. It is only 4 m above sea level. 
Vikingavatn was an e

attractions.name: Heidarspordar
attractions.extract: The Heiðarsporðar volcanic system is located in northeast Iceland near Lake Mývatn. It is the source of numerous formations present in and around the lake, particularly in the form of pseudocraters.

attractions.name: Frostastaðavatn
attractions.extract: Frostastaðavatn is a lake in Iceland. It is situated in the Highlands of Iceland, not far from the famous mountains of Landmannalaugar and the volcano Hekla.

attractions.name: Hóp
attractions.extract: The lake Hóp is situated in the north of Iceland near Blönduós at the Húnafjörður.

attractions.name: Helgafell
attractions.extract: Helgafell is an inactive 227 m (745 ft) volcanic cone located on the island of Heimaey in the Vestmannaeyjar archipelago in Iceland.

attractions.name: Rauðhólar
attractions.extract: The Rauðhólar are remnants of a cluster of rootless cones in Elliðaárhraun lava fields on the south-eastern outskirts of Reykjavík, Iceland next to the South Iceland part of Hringvegur, the Suðurlandsvegur.

attractions.name: Safnasafnid Icelandic Folk and Outsider Art Museum
attractions.extract: The Icelandic Folk and Outsider Art Museum is a museum of folk and outsider art located outside Akureyri in northern Iceland. It was founded in 1995 by artist Níels Hafstein, and Magnhildur Sigurðardóttir, a retired psychiatric nurse.

attractions.name: Esja
attractions.extract: Esja is a 914 m (2,999 ft) mountain situated in the south-west of Iceland, about ten kilometres north of Iceland's capital city Reykjavík.

attractions.name: Litlanesfoss
attractions.extract: Litlanesfoss is a waterfall in Hengifossá in Fljótsdalur, Eastern Iceland, also known as Stuðlabergsfoss. The waterfall is about 30 meters high and forms an apron in a cliff. The waterfall is in a large rock choir with an unusually regular supporting rock made of high and straight columns.

attractions.name: Litli-Hrútur
attractions.extract: Fagradalsfjall is an active tuya volcano formed in the Last Glacial Period on the Reykjanes Peninsula, around 40 kilometres (25 mi) from Reykjavík, Iceland. Fagradalsfjall is also the name for the wider volcanic system covering an area 5 kilometres (3 mi) wide and 16 kilometres (10 mi) long between

attractions.name: Súlur
attractions.extract: Súlur is a mountain located to the south west of the town Akureyri in Iceland rising some 1213 meters. It is popular for hiking tours.

attractions.name: Sveifluháls
attractions.extract: Sveifluháls is a mafic hyaloclastite ridge of 397 m height in the southwest of Iceland in Gullbringusýsla. It is part of Krýsuvík volcanic system and of the protected area Reykjanes Fólkvangur.

attractions.name: Hvítavatn
attractions.extract: Iceland has over 20 lakes larger than 10 km2, and at least 40 others varying between 2.5 and 10 km2 in size. This list also includes a few smaller lakes and ponds that are considered notable. The figures for many of the smaller lakes are unreliable. Also, some larger lakes vary considerably in size

attractions.name: Vífilsfell
attractions.extract: Vífilsfell is a hyaloclastite ridge in southwestern Iceland (Weichselian). It is 655 m high and located west of Jósepsdalur valley on the volcanic plateau of Hellisheiði. It forms the northernmost offset of the Bláfjöll mountain massif and is situated on top of the fissure system of Brennisteinsfjöl

attractions.name: Dyngjujökull
attractions.extract: Dyngjujökull is an outlet glacier of the Vatnajökull glacier in Vatnajökull National Park, Iceland. The glacier is in the Icelandic Highlands and is situated between Bárðarbunga, Holuhraun and Kverkfjöll. Meltwater from the glacier flows into Jökulsá á Fjöllum, the second longest river in Iceland.

attractions.name: Eiríksjökull
attractions.extract: Eiríksjökull is a glacier north-west of Langjökull in Iceland, with an area of 22 km2 (8.5 sq mi) reaching a height of 1,675 m (5,495 ft), atop the largest table mountain in Iceland which goes by the same name.

attractions.name: Hafursey
attractions.extract: Hafursey is an inselberg in Southern Iceland. It has a length of 4.8 km (3.0 mi) and a width of 3 km (1.9 mi).

attractions.name: Loki-Fögrufjöll
attractions.extract: The Loki-Fögrufjöll is a subglacial volcano under the Vatnajökull glacier.

attractions.name: Elliðavatn
attractions.extract: Elliðavatn is a lake in Iceland. It is situated in the area of Reykjavík.

attractions.name: Hellnahellir
attractions.extract: Hellnahellir is the largest man-made or artificial cave in Iceland. It is located in Landsveit, South Iceland. The cave is almost 50 metres (160 ft) long and the walls are covered with engravings. Its age is unknown but it is most probably from 12th or 13th century.

attractions.name: Esjufjöll
attractions.extract: The subglacial Esjufjöll volcano is located in the south-east part of the Vatnajökull icecap. Esjufjöll is a strict nature reserve.

attractions.name: Fagrifoss
attractions.extract: Fagrifoss is a waterfall located in the Highland in Iceland. It is almost on the road leading to Lakagígar craters on road F206.24 km (15 mi) from Kirkjubæjarklaustur and 40 km (25 mi) from Laki. Access to the waterfall requires the crossing of a river ford, for which a 4x4 vehicle is needed.

attractions.name: Laugarvatn
attractions.extract: Laugarvatn is the name of a lake and a small town in the south of Iceland. The lake is smaller than the neighbouring Apavatn.

attractions.name: Hengill
attractions.extract: Hengill is a volcanic table mountain situated in the south-west of Iceland, to the south of Þingvellir.

attractions.name: Hvítárvatn
attractions.extract: Hvítárvatn is a lake in the Highlands of Iceland and the source of the glacial river Hvítá, Árnessýsla. It is located 45 kilometres (28 mi) northeast of Gullfoss waterfall. Its surface is about 30 square kilometres (12 sq mi) and its greatest depth is 84 metres (276 ft). It lies at an elevation of 4

attractions.name: Selfoss
attractions.extract: Selfoss is a waterfall on the river Jökulsá á Fjöllum in the north of Iceland. The river drops over a number of waterfalls over about 30 km before flowing into Öxarfjörður, a bay of the Arctic Sea. The river originates as melt water from the glacier Vatnajökull and therefore the water flow varies de

attractions.name: Tindfjallajökull
attractions.extract: Tindfjallajökull is a glacier in the south of Iceland whose name is also given to the underlying stratovolcano. Tindfjöll is a ridge that extends to the south of the glacier and is an alternative name for the volcano. The name of the glacier in Icelandic means "Tindfjöll glacier".

attractions.name: Kverkfjöll
attractions.extract: Kverkfjöll is a potentially active central volcano, fissure swarm, and associated mountain range situated on the northern border of the glacier Vatnajökull in Iceland.

attractions.name: Vesturhópsvatn
attractions.extract: Vesturhópsvatn is a lake in northern Iceland. It is located in the municipality of Húnaþing vestra.

attractions.name: Torfajökull
attractions.extract: Torfajökull is a rhyolitic stratovolcano, with a large caldera capped by a glacier of the same name and associated with a complex of subglacial volcanoes. Torfajökull last erupted in 1477 and consists of the largest area of silicic extrusive rocks in Iceland. This is now known to be due to a VEI 5 e

attractions.name: Snæfell
attractions.extract: Snæfell at 1,833 m (6,014 ft) high, is an ice-capped stratovolcano located in the north-east part of Vatnajökull National Park, Iceland. While it has been dormant in the Holocene, it is now known to have had repose times of over 100,000 years between eruptions, so it cannot be assumed to be extinct.

attractions.name: Hágöngulón
attractions.extract: Hágöngulón is a lake in the Highlands of Iceland. It is 37 square kilometres (14 mi2) in area and it is situated nearly 10 kilometres (6.2 mi) east of the Sprengisandur road, one of the highland tracks which cross Iceland from the south to the north, and a similar distance west of the largest glacie

attractions.name: Geitlandsjökull
attractions.extract: Geitlandsjökull is a lateral glacier of Langjökull, with a flowshed of 13 km2 (5.0 sq mi) from the second largest ice cap in Iceland at 904 km2 (349 sq mi), in the west of Iceland. It has also been used as the name for the substantial Langjökull ice cap, as has the name Suðurjöklar. The highest poin

attractions.name: Búri
attractions.extract: The Búri cave is a lava tube located in southwestern Iceland and was discovered in 1992, by the volcanologist Guðmundur Þorsteinsson.

attractions.name: Hvalvatn
attractions.extract: The lake Hvalvatn is situated east of Hvalfjörður in the western portion of Iceland. It lies to the east of the tuya Hvalfell. The surface measures 4.1 km2 (1.6 sq mi), and its greatest depth is 180 m (590 ft).

attractions.name: Grensdalur
attractions.extract:
Grensdalur is a central volcanic vent and mountain of the Hengill volcanic system in Iceland. The elevation is about 497 m (1631 ft). Its position is 64.02°N 21.17°W.
It was active during Pleistocene.

attractions.name: Urriðafoss
attractions.extract: Urriðafoss is a waterfall located in the river Þjórsá in southwest Iceland. With a flow rate of 360 m³/s, it has the highest average water flow of any waterfall in Iceland, and the second highest in Europe behind the Rheinfall.

attractions.name: Grímsnes
attractions.extract: Grímsnes is a relatively small fissure or crater row volcanic system located in South Iceland, located south–east of Lake Thingvallavatn and east of the en echelon group of volcanic systems extending across the Reykjanes Peninsula, that erupted last in the Holocene.

attractions.name: Guðrúnarlaug
attractions.extract: Guðrúnarlaug is a thermal bath in Iceland near Sælingsdalur in the municipality of Dalabyggð. The name stems from Guðrún Ósvífrsdóttir, who is a main character in Laxdæla saga. The thermal bath exists at the place described in the saga.

attractions.name: Hafragilsfoss
attractions.extract: Hafragilsfoss is a waterfall in Iceland.

attractions.name: Hafravatn
attractions.extract: Hafravatn is a small lake on the eastern outskirts of Reykjavík, Iceland. Located at 76 m above sea level, it has an area of 1.08 km2 (0.42 sq mi) with a greatest depth of 28 m (92 ft). The Seljadalsá River flows into it from the east and its discharge is Úlfarsá. A small village lies on the norther

attractions.name: Fláajökull
attractions.extract: Fláajökull is a smaller, slow flowing glacier of Iceland, located on the east side of volcano Breiðabunga in the east of Iceland, in Vatnajökull National Park, 40 km on road or 20 km on the map, northwest of Höfn town.

attractions.name: Landmannahellir
attractions.extract: Landmannahellir is a small cave at the foot of the Hellisfjall mountain middle of Suðurland in the influence area of the volcano Hekla.

attractions.name: Helgafell
attractions.extract: Helgafell is a mountain on Reykjanes peninsula, Iceland. The height of the mountain is 338 m.

attractions.name: Helgrindur
attractions.extract: Helgrindur is a volcanic mountain range or massif in the middle of the Snæfellsnes peninsula that provides a backdrop to the port of Grundarfjörður. The volcano can be regarded as potentially active, with a risk of lava flows and much more rarely explosive tephra eruptions. The range with its promin

attractions.name: Hlöðufell
attractions.extract: Hlöðufell is a tuya volcano, located about 10 km southwest of Langjökull, Iceland. Hlöðufell is 1188 metres above sea level, and was formed when lava erupted through Langjökull during the Pleistocene. The tuya also has the characteristics of a composite volcano.

attractions.name: Stóra-Björnsfell
attractions.extract: Stóra-Björnsfell is an elongated medium-sized tuya located in Iceland. It contains pillow lava, hyaloclastite and sheet lava. The volcano formed when a subglacial eruption occurred beneath an ice sheet during the last ice age. Stóra-Björnsfell's pillow lava appears originally to have erupted from a

attractions.name: Hjörleifshöfði
attractions.extract: Hjörleifshöfði is a 221 m (725 ft)-high inselberg in southern Iceland. It consists of palagonite. The mountain is located on the Mýrdalssandur outwash plain about 15 km (9.3 mi) east of Vík í Mýrdal, and was an island in the Atlantic Ocean.

attractions.name: The Árnesinga Folk Museum
attractions.extract: The Árnesinga Folk Museum is a small museum located at Eyrarbakki, a village on the south coast of Iceland, where visitors can experience past times and learn about the history of the building and the region. It is situated in the center of the village near the church. It is also accessible by Stræt

attractions.name: Hrómundartindur
attractions.extract: Hrómundartindur is a mountain in Iceland north of Hveragerði with an elevation of 540 metres (1,770 ft). It to the east of Hengill and is the central volcano of an adjacent 25 km (16 mi) long Hrómundartindur volcanic system, which contains the Ölkeduháls geothermal field. Like Hengill this area is c

attractions.name: Akranes Museum Centre
attractions.extract: The Akranes Folk Museum, formerly known as the Akranes Museum Centre, consists of a folk museum, an exhibition hall and a few 19th century buildings and boats in Garðar, Akranes, Iceland. The museum houses a few exhibitions, as well as a large open-air museum, which consists mainly of houses and bo

attractions.name: Prestahnúkur
attractions.extract: The peak Prestahnúkur with a height of 1,223 m (4,012 ft), is in the Western Volcanic Zone to the west of the Highlands of Iceland to the west of Langjökull glacier, or to be more specific, to the west of Geitlandsjökull glacier, a part of the Langjökull. The volcano Prestahnúkur includes the terrai

attractions.name: Hverfjall
attractions.extract: Hverfjall is a tephra cone or tuff ring volcano in northern Iceland, to the east of Mývatn.

attractions.name: Lómagnúpur
attractions.extract: Lómagnúpur is a subglacial mound in southern Iceland.

attractions.name: Leitin
attractions.extract: Leitin is a small shield volcano in the southwest of Iceland.

attractions.name: Þverárfjall
attractions.extract: Þverárfjall is a mountain in Austur-Húnavatnssýsla, Iceland between Norðurárdalur valley, which runs to the northwest from what is often called "front Laxárdalur" in Austur-Húnavatnssýsla and the Laxárdalur in Skagafjörður. The mountain is named after Þverá, the innermost town in Norðurárdalur valle

attractions.name: Miklavatn
attractions.extract: Miklavatn is a lake in Fljót in Skagafjörður, Iceland. It is the second biggest lake in the region at 7.4 square kilometers. The Hraunamöl isthmus separates it from the sea, but the runoff from the lake flows through the Hraunaós estuary. The lake was originally a fjord but as the isthmus formed, it

attractions.name: Miklavatn
attractions.extract: Miklavatn in Borgarsveit is a lake in Skagafjörður, Iceland, near the head of the west side of the fjord. The lake is oblong and lies parallel to the western Héraðsvötn, narrowing slightly in the north. From the northern end, there is runoff into the Héraðsvötn. It is called Víkin, and to the north

attractions.name: Molduxi
attractions.extract: Molduxi is a mountain overlooking Sauðárkrókur and Borgarsveit in Skagafjörður, Iceland. The mountain is 706 metres (2,316 ft) and easy to climb. There is a panoramic view of Skagafjörður from the top of Molduxi, and there is a popular hiking trail that leads up the mountain from Sauðárkrókur.

attractions.name: Rauðavatn
attractions.extract: Lake Rauðavatn is a lake on the eastern side of Reykjavík, north of Elliðavatn. The Route 1 ring road passes the lake to the west, separating it from the suburb of Árbær.

attractions.name: Stórhöfði
attractions.extract: Stórhöfði is a peninsula and the southernmost point of Heimaey, the largest island in the Vestmannaeyjar archipelago, in Iceland. It is claimed to be the windiest place in Europe, and holds the record for the lowest on land observation of air pressure in Europe. The name means great cape and is also

attractions.name: Technical Museum of East Iceland
attractions.extract: The Technical Museum of East Iceland was formally established in 1984, and its first exhibition opened in 1995 on the centenary of the town of Seyðisfjörður. The museum focuses on the history of Iceland's industrialization and technological advancement from roughly 1880 to the present day. Its core

attractions.name: Ingólfsfjall
attractions.extract: Ingólfsfjall is a 551-metre-high (1,808 ft) tuya in Iceland in the vicinity of Hveragerði.

attractions.name: Westfjords Heritage Museum
attractions.extract: The Westfjords Heritage Museum is a museum in Ísafjörður in the Westfjords of Iceland.

attractions.name: The Icelandic Museum of Rock 'n' Roll
attractions.extract: The Icelandic Museum of Rock 'n' Roll is located at the Hljómahöll concert and conference hall in Reykjanesbær. It was formally opened on April 5, 2014.

attractions.name: Keilir
attractions.extract: Keilir is a Pleistocene subglacial mound or perhaps a conical tuya on Reykjanes Peninsula in Iceland. Basal area is 0.773 km2, summit area 0.004 km2, basal width 0.99 km, summit width 0.07 km, volume 0.0362 km3.

attractions.name: Kerlingarfjöll
attractions.extract: Kerlingarfjöll is a 1,477 m (4,846 ft) tall volcanic massif in Iceland situated in the Highlands of Iceland near the Kjölur highland road. It is usually regarded as part of a large tuya fissure system of 100 km2 (39 sq mi) in the southern portion of the Hofsjökull volcanic system, although is about

attractions.name: Búrfell
attractions.extract: Búrfell and the connected lava channel Búrfellsgjá are famous and protected landmarks of the Reykjanes peninsula, Southwest Iceland. They are part of the Krýsuvík volcanic system.

attractions.name: Surtshellir
attractions.extract: Surtshellir is a lava cave located in western Iceland, around 60 km from the settlement of Borgarnes. Approximately a mile in length, it is one of the longest such caves in the country. It was the first known lava tube in the world, at least by modern speleologists, and remained the longest known la

attractions.name: Hoffellsjökull
attractions.extract: Hoffellsjökull is an outlet glacier which flows from the ice cap of Vatnajökull. It is located in the municipality of Hornafjörður, southeast Iceland. Hoffellsjökull takes its name from Hoffell; a mountainous area and a farmland. Hoffellsjökull and the Hoffell area are a part of Vatnajökull National

attractions.name: Grímsvötn
attractions.extract: Grímsvötn is an active volcano with a fissure system located in Vatnajökull National Park, Iceland. The central volcano is completely subglacial and located under the northwestern side of the Vatnajökull ice cap. The subglacial caldera is at 64°25′N 17°20′W, at an elevation of 1,725 m (5,659 ft). Be

attractions.name: Hekla
attractions.extract: Hekla, or Hecla, is an active stratovolcano in the south of Iceland with a height of 1,491 m (4,892 ft). Hekla is one of Iceland's most active volcanoes; over 20 eruptions have occurred in and around the volcano since the year 1210. During the Middle Ages, the Icelandic Norse called the volcano the

attractions.name: Loðmundur
attractions.extract: Loðmundur is a 1,432 m (4,700 ft) high mountain in the Kerlingarfjöll mountain range in Iceland. It stands by itself on the northeast edge of the range. The hills are very steep and the top section is surrounded by near-vertical cliffs. The top itself is flat once reached.

attractions.name: Álafoss
attractions.extract: Álafoss is a waterfall on the river Varmá in Mosfellsbær, Iceland.

attractions.name: Ok
attractions.extract: Ok is a shield volcano in Iceland, to the west of Langjökull. It erupted during interglacials in the Pleistocene, and is in proximity to the Prestahnúkur and Oddnýjarhnjúkur-Langjökull volcanic systems. The volcano was once topped by the Okjökull glacier, which may now only be represented by isolate

attractions.name: Katla
attractions.extract: Katla is an active subglacial volcano in southern Iceland. This volcano has been very active historically with at least twenty documented major eruptions since 2920 BC. In its recent history though, Katla has been less active as the last major eruption occurred in 1918. These eruptions have had a Vo

attractions.name: Trölladyngja
attractions.extract: Situated in the Ódáðahraun lava field, Trölladyngja is the biggest of the Icelandic shield volcanoes, reaching a height of 1,460 metres (4,790 ft) above sea level, and rising almost 600 m (2,000 ft) above the surrounding desert and lava fields. It part of the Bárðarbunga volcanic system and has a vo

attractions.name: Þjófafoss
attractions.extract: Þjófafoss is located on the river Þjórsá on the east side of the Merkurhraun lava fields in the south of Iceland, at the southwest tip of the hill Búrfell. A viewing point for the waterfall can be accessed by a gravel track that leads about 4 kilometres (2.5 mi) northwest from Route 26 or by a track

attractions.name: The Transportation Museum at Ystafell
attractions.extract: The Transportation Museum at Ystafell is the oldest transportation museum in Iceland, established in 1998 and opened in July 2000. Located between Akureyri (~57 km) and Húsavík (~37 km) it sits in the valley Kaldakinn. The museum features a collection of historic cars and trucks, and displays about

attractions.name: Askja
attractions.extract: Askja ( ) is an active volcano situated in a remote part of the central highlands of Iceland. The name Askja refers to a complex of nested calderas within the surrounding Dyngjufjöll mountains, which rise to 1,514 m (4,967 ft), askja meaning box or caldera in Icelandic.

attractions.name: Mývatn
attractions.extract:
Mývatn is a shallow lake situated in an area of active volcanism in the north of Iceland, near Krafla volcano. It has a high amount of biological activity. The lake and the surrounding wetlands provide a habitat for a number of waterbirds, especially ducks.
The lake was created by a large basaltic l

attractions.name: Rauðhólar
attractions.extract: Rauðhólar (Vesturdalur) is a small chain of volcanoes within the Askja or the Fremrinámur volcanic systems in the north of Iceland.

attractions.name: Merkjárfoss
attractions.extract: Gluggafoss is a waterfall in southern Iceland, specifically in the Fljótshlíð area. As the most prominent member of a series of waterfalls running from the river Merkjá, it is also known as Merkjárfoss ; both names are acknowledged on an on-site signpost. The waterfall is accessible off Route 261, s

attractions.name: Gljúfrasteinn
attractions.extract: Gljúfrasteinn is a writer's home museum, which was the former home of Halldór Kiljan Laxness, a 1955 Nobel Prize for Literature winner. It is located in Mosfellsbær, east of Reykjavík, Iceland. The name of the house is derived from a large stone in the vicinity called Gljúfrasteinn, about which Hall

attractions.name: Maelifell
attractions.extract: Mælifell is a volcano located in Southern Iceland.

attractions.name: Reykjanes (volcanic system)
attractions.extract: Reykjanes is a small headland on the south-western end of the Reykjanes Peninsula in Iceland, giving the main peninsula its name. Volcanic action is responsible for forming the entire peninsula. The nearest town is Keflavik.

attractions.name: Vatnaöldur
attractions.extract: Vatnaöldur is the name of a series of craters in the Suðurland region of Iceland. They are located in the Highlands of Iceland, north–west of the Veiðivötn and north–east of Landmannalaugar, within the municipality of Rangárþing ytra. It is part of the Eastern volcanic zone (EVZ).

attractions.name: Uxatindar
attractions.extract: The Highland, also known as the Central Highland, is an area that comprises much of the interior land of Iceland. The Highland is situated above 300–400 meters (980–1,310 ft) and is mostly uninhabitable. The soil is primarily volcanic ash, and the terrain consists of basalt mountains and lava fields

attractions.name: Tindastóll
attractions.extract: Tindastóll is a 989-meter-tall (3,245-foot) mountain located on the far west side of Skagafjörður County, Iceland and to the north of Sauðárkrókur. It is one of the best-known mountains in the region, and it provides an excellent view in clear weather. The name of the mountain is often shortened to

attractions.name: Glóðafeykir
attractions.extract: Glóðafeykir is a mountain in eastern Skagafjörður, Iceland in the middle of the Blönduhlíð mountain range, directly opposite Varmahlíð. Its shape is gabled and it is rather striking. The mountain is rocky towards the top, but is still fairly easy to hike. Glóðafeykir, also often called Feykir or Fey

attractions.name: Fagradalsfjall
attractions.extract: Fagradalsfjall is an active tuya volcano formed in the Last Glacial Period on the Reykjanes Peninsula, around 40 kilometres (25 mi) from Reykjavík, Iceland. Fagradalsfjall is also the name for the wider volcanic system covering an area 5 kilometres (3 mi) wide and 16 kilometres (10 mi) long between

attractions.name: Faxi
attractions.extract: The Faxi waterfall is located on the Golden Circle, a popular tourist trail approximately 120 km east of Reykjavik. The waterfall is located on the Tungufljót river, a tributary of the river Hvita.

attractions.name: Brennisteinsalda
attractions.extract: The Brennisteinsalda is a volcano in the south of Iceland. Its height is about 855 m. It is situated near Landmannalaugar and not far from Hekla.

attractions.name: Langisjór
attractions.extract: Langisjór is a lake in the western part of Vatnajökull National Park, Iceland. It is around 20 km in length and up to 2 km wide, with a total surface area of about 26 km2 (10 sq mi) and a depth of 75m at its deepest point.

attractions.name: Thridrangaviti Lighthouse
attractions.extract: Þrídrangaviti Lighthouse is an active lighthouse 7.2 kilometres off the southwest coast of Iceland, in the archipelago of Vestmannaeyjar. It is often described as one of the most isolated lighthouses in the world. Þrídrangar means "three rock pillars", referring to the three named sea stacks at that

attractions.name: The Caves of Ægissíða
attractions.extract: The Caves of Hella are a series of ancient man-made sandstone caves located at the farm Ægissíða on the bank of the river Ytri-Rangá, just across from the village Hella, in the southern part of Iceland. Through the past centuries the caves have been used by Icelandic farmers as sheep sheds, barns an

attractions.name: Ófærufoss
attractions.extract: Ófærufoss is a waterfall situated in the Eldgjá chasm in the western part of Vatnajökull National Park, Iceland. Until the early 1990s a natural bridge spanned the falls, but it collapsed from natural causes.

attractions.name: Öræfajökull
attractions.extract: Öræfajökull is an ice-covered volcano in south-east Iceland. The largest active volcano and the highest peak in Iceland at 2,110 metres (6,920 ft), it lies within the Vatnajökull National Park and is covered by part of the glacier.

attractions.name: Öxarárfoss
attractions.extract: Öxarárfoss is a waterfall in Þingvellir National Park, Iceland. It flows from the river Öxará over the Almannagjá. The pool at the base of the waterfall is filled with rocks and is often extremely icy during winter.

attractions.name: Hlíðarfjall
attractions.extract: Hlíðarfjall is a mountain and ski resort located around 6 km west of the Icelandic town Akureyri in Eyjafjörður. The mountain itself has an elevation of 1,200m, It is the second largest ski resort in Iceland and reaches a top elevation of 1,014m. The base of the resort is at an elevation of 500m.

attractions.name: Hrútsfjallstindar
attractions.extract: Vatnajökull is the largest and most voluminous ice cap in Iceland, and the second largest in area in Europe after the Severny Island ice cap of Novaya Zemlya. It is in the south-east of the island, covering approximately 8% of the country.

attractions.name: Úlfarsfell
attractions.extract: Úlfarsfell is a mountain in the municipality of Mosfellsbær in the Reykjavík metropolitan area. It is 296 meters high. The mountain slopes have been subject to extensive forestry.

attractions.name: Úlfljótsvatn
attractions.extract: Úlfljótsvatn is a lake in southern Iceland, to the south of lake Þingvallavatn, 74km east of Reykjavík. Úlfljótsvatn is named after Úlfljótr, an important man who was involved in the Alþingi in 930.

attractions.name: Morsárfoss
attractions.extract: Morsárfoss is a waterfall in Vatnajökull National Park, Iceland. Measurements showed that one of the several waterfalls in the area is at least 240 metres (790 ft) in total height, making it the tallest waterfall in Iceland. Morsárfoss became visible in 2007 when Morsárjökull, an outlet glacier of V

attractions.name: Mælifellshnjúkur
attractions.extract: Mælifellshnjúkur is a mountain on the inner western side of Skagafjörður, Iceland. It is 1,138 meters tall, It is one of the most prominent and best-known of Skagafjörður's mountains, towering over those nearby. The peak is allegedly visible from ten counties. It also offers a good view, and is a po

attractions.name: Þeistareykjarbunga
attractions.extract: Theistareykjarbunga is a shield volcano in north-eastern Iceland with two fissure vents called Þeistareykjahraun and Borgahraun, and two cones: the 370 m (1,210 ft) Stórahversmór and the 540 m (1,770 ft), 30 km3 (7.2 cu mi) Stóravíti. They both are currently extinct, although there is evidence sugge

attractions.name: Þingvallavatn
attractions.extract: Þingvallavatn, anglicised as Thingvallavatn, is a rift valley lake in southwestern Iceland. With a surface of 84 km2 (32 sq mi) it is the largest natural lake in Iceland. Its greatest depth is 114 m (374 ft). At the northern shore of the lake, at Þingvellir, the Alþingi, the national parliament, was

attractions.name: Þorbjörn
attractions.extract: Þorbjörn is a 243 m high volcanic mountain next to the town of Grindavík (Gullbringusýsla) on Reykjanes peninsula, Iceland. Blue Lagoon can be easily seen from the summit.

attractions.name: Þríhnúkagígur
attractions.extract: Þríhnúkagígur is a dormant volcano in the volcanic system of Brennisteinsfjöll near Reykjavík, Iceland. Covering a 3,270 square metres (35,200 sq ft) area and a depth of 213 meters (699 ft), it has not erupted in the past 4000 years. It was discovered in 1974 by cave explorer Árni B. Stefánsson, and

attractions.name: Þórisjökull
attractions.extract: Þórisjökull or Thórisjökull is a small glacier and volcano in western-central Iceland, to the southwest of Langjökull glacier. It has an elevation of 1,350 metres (4,430 ft). Kaldidalur lies in the foreground.

attractions.name: Æðarstein lighthouse
attractions.extract: The Æðarstein Lighthouse is located on the southeast coast of Iceland, on a rocky point on the west side of the port of Djúpivogur.

attractions.name: Garðskagaviti
attractions.extract: Garðskagaviti is a lighthouse located on Garðskaði, the easternmost tip of Iceland's Southern Peninsula. Garðskagaviti actually refers to two lighthouses: the first was built in 1897, and in 1944, a second lighthouse was built to replace the original. The newer lighthouse has been home to the Herita

attractions.name: Hraunhafnartangi lighthouse
attractions.extract: Hraunhafnartangi Lighthouse is the northernmost lighthouse in Iceland. It is located about 800 meters south of the Arctic Circle.

attractions.name: Knarrarós Lighthouse
attractions.extract: The Knarrarós Lighthouse is located on the south coast of Iceland.

attractions.name: Búrfellshyrna
attractions.extract: Búrfellshyrna is a mountain in the Svarfaðardalur in northern Iceland. It is made of basaltic layers of Tertiary age. It reaches 1,091 meters at its highest peak. It is situated between the valleys of Búrfellsdalur and Grýtudalur. In Búrfellsdalur is the Búrfell Glacier, which exhibits what is somet

attractions.name: Árbæjarsafn
attractions.extract: Árbæjarsafn is the historical museum of the city of Reykjavík as well as an open-air museum and a regional museum. Its purpose is to give the public an insight into the living conditions, work and recreational activities of the people of Reykjavík in earlier times.

attractions.name: Eldgjá
attractions.extract: Eldgjá is a volcano and a canyon in Iceland. Eldgjá is part of the Katla volcano; it is a segment of a 40 kilometres (25 mi) long chain of volcanic craters and fissure vents that extends northeast away from Katla volcano almost to the Vatnajökull ice cap. This fissure experienced a major eruption ar

attractions.name: Apavatn
attractions.extract: Apavatn is a lake in southwest Iceland. With a surface area of around 13 km2 it is much larger than the neighbouring lake of Laugarvatn, which lies to the north of Apavatn.

attractions.name: Bolafjall
attractions.extract: Bolafjall is a 638 meter high mountain in the vicinity of Bolungarvík. A road was built to the top in 1986. In 2002 it was opened to civilian traffic. In 2022, an observation deck with a view over the Ísafjarðardjúp was opened on the mountain.

attractions.name: Viking World museum
attractions.extract: Viking World is a museum in Njarðvík, Reykjanesbær, Iceland.

attractions.name: Hreðavatn
attractions.extract: Hreðavatn is a lake in the west of Iceland. It is situated near Route 1 between Borgarnes and the pass of Holtavörtuheiði. Nearby is the university faculty of the Bifröst University and the Grábrók craters as well as the mountain Baula.

attractions.name: Leirvogsvatn
attractions.extract: Leirvogsvatn is a small lake, located about 23 kilometres (14 mi) northeast of Reykjavík, Iceland. It lies along Route 36. Located at 211 metres (692 ft) above sea level, it has an area of 1.2 square kilometres (0.46 sq mi) with a maximum depth of 16 metres (52 ft). Fed by the Leirvogsvatn River, th

attractions.name: Baula
attractions.extract: Baula is a mountain situated in the west of Iceland near Route 1, Bifröst University, and the craters of Grábrók. The mountain's reddish or orange hue is caused by its rhyolite rock composition.

attractions.name: Okjökull
attractions.extract: Okjökull was a glacier in western Iceland on top of the shield volcano Ok.

attractions.name: Icelandic Museum of Design and Applied Art
attractions.extract: The Icelandic Museum of Design and Applied Art is a museum of product and furniture design in the town Garðabær, near Reykjavík in Iceland. The collection was started in 1998, but the museum did not open until 2010. It has a permanent exhibition consisting mainly of Scandinavian design objects of th

attractions.name: Nyrðra-Vatnalautavatn
attractions.extract: Nyrðra-Vatnalautavatn is the largest body of water on Ófeigsfjarðarheiði in Iceland. It is located in the Árneshreppur district in the Westfjords.

attractions.name: Krafla
attractions.extract: Krafla is a volcanic caldera of about 10 km (6.2 mi) in diameter with a 90 km (56 mi) long fissure zone. It is located in the north of Iceland in the Mývatn region and is situated on the Iceland hotspot atop the Mid-Atlantic Ridge, which forms the divergent boundary between the North American Plate

attractions.name: Krýsuvík
attractions.extract: Krýsuvík is an area in Southwest Iceland at about 35 km from Reykjavík.

attractions.name: Thordarhyrna
attractions.extract: Thordarhyrna is one of seven subglacial volcanoes beneath the Vatnajokull glacier in Iceland. It is a paired active central volcano with Grímsvötn, and can be classified as part of the Grímsvötn-Laki volcanic system, with common fissure swarms to the south.

attractions.name: Vörðufell
attractions.extract: Vörðufell is a 391 metres (1,283 ft) mountain near the Hvítá river and the town of Iða in Árnessýsla, Iceland. Geologically, the mountain consists primarily of tuff and diabase. From the air, Vörðufell appears triangular with the small lake, Úlfsvatn, near the summit.

attractions.name: Óshyrna
attractions.extract: Óshyrna is a mountain in the Westfjords in Iceland. It is the outermost part of the mountain slope between Ísafjörður and Bolungarvík. The old road to Bolungarvík lies between Óshyrna and the ocean. The Óshlíð road is one of Iceland's most dangerous roads because of frequent avalanches and rock fall

attractions.name: Þverfellshorn
attractions.extract:
Þverfellshorn is a peak in the Esjan mountains of Capital Region in southwestern Iceland. It is located roughly 15 kilometres (9.3 mi) by air northeast of Reykjavík. It is one of the most visited peaks in Iceland, attracting hikers for its scenic views.
Its elevation range is 820 metres (2,690 ft) -

attractions.name: Fremrinámar
attractions.extract:
Fremrinámur is a volcano with a volcanic system located on the basalt plateau in Iceland. It is at the junction of the Mid-Atlantic Ridge and the Greenland–Iceland–Faeroe Ridge. It is one of five volcanic systems found in the axial rift zone in north-east Iceland.
Its height is about 939 m (3,081 ft

attractions.name: Kerling
attractions.extract: Mount Kerling is the highest mountain in the District of Eyjafjarðarsveit, in northern Iceland. It is only 13 km SSW of Akureyri. Its structure is mainly basaltic rock, but the uppermost part is rhyolite, which extends to Súlur mountain nearby and all the way around the head of the Glerárdalur valle

attractions.name: Brennisteinsfjöll
attractions.extract: Brennisteinsfjöll is a minor volcanic system, with crater rows and small shield volcanoes on the Reykjanes Peninsula in southwest Iceland.

attractions.name: Akrafjall
attractions.extract: Akrafjall is a volcanic mountain in western Iceland. It is located in the municipality of Hvalfjarðarsveit, east of the city of Akranes.

attractions.name: Búlandstindur
attractions.extract: Búlandstindur is a mountain in Eastern Iceland between the bays Berufjörður and Hamarsfjörður. Mt. Búlandstindur is 1,069 metres (3,507 ft) above sea level. It is a pyramid-shaped stack of basaltic strata. The villages around this mountain are famous for fishing.

attractions.name: Bláhnjúkur
attractions.extract: Bláhnjúkur is a volcano in the south of Iceland. Its height is 945 m.

attractions.name: Borgarvirki
attractions.extract: Borgarvirki lies between Vesturhóp and Víðidalur in the north of Iceland, and at 177m above sea level it dominates the surrounding region. Made out of basalt strata, it has been used as a fortress. Borgarvirki is a natural phenomenon, altered by humans in earlier centuries. In 1949, Borgarvirki was

attractions.name: Breiðamerkurjökull
attractions.extract: Breiðamerkurjökull is an outlet glacier of the larger glacier of Vatnajökull and a piedmont glacier in the southern part of Vatnajökull National Park, Iceland. Emerging as a tongue of the Vatnajökull, it ends in two lagoons, famous Jökulsárlón and smaller and less known Breiðárlón. Over time, it has

attractions.name: Höfðavatn
attractions.extract: Höfðavatn is a lagoon-like lake in northern Iceland. It is located in the municipality of Skagafjörður.

attractions.name: Grænalón
attractions.extract: Grænalón was one of the glacial lakes of the Icelandic glacier Vatnajökull. It was situated in the south of Iceland. Its surface measured 18 km2 during the 20th Century.

attractions.name: Herðubreið
attractions.extract: Herðubreið is a tuya in the northern part of Vatnajökull National Park, Iceland. It is situated in the Highlands of Iceland at the east side of the Ódáðahraun desert and close to Askja volcano. The desert is a large lava field originating from eruptions of Trölladyngja and other shield volcanoes in

attractions.name: Reynisvatn
attractions.extract: Reynisvatn is a lake on the eastern side of Reykjavík, east of Grafarholt og Úlfarsárdalur. The lake is a popular fishing spot, with rainbow trout being released.

attractions.name: Helgafell
attractions.extract:
Helgafell is a small mountain on the Snæfellsnes Peninsula of Iceland. The mountain is 73 metres (240 ft) high. A temple in honor of Thor (Þór) was built there by Þórólfr Mostrarskegg, the first settler of the area. 
His biography is described in literary form with fictional and mythical elements in

attractions.name: Krýsuvík
attractions.extract: The volcanic system of Krýsuvík, is situated in the south–west of Iceland on the Reykjanes peninsula. It is located in the middle of Reykjanes and on the divergent plate boundary of the Mid-Atlantic Ridge which traverses Iceland. It was named after the Krýsuvík area which is part of it and consists
